unresolved_items: reviewer-codex:group-1: Terminal-state evidence is still not canonical after the merged repair flow: final-round ultimatum edits and PM-003 counters diverge from the machine-facing artifacts that are supposed to describe them.
reviewer-codex:group-2: The merged revision flow does not fail closed when required fix-items evidence is missing, so round 1+ can silently drop the review negotiation contract.

reviewer-claude:group-1: run-postmortem.json PM-003 is still non-canonical across its two writers. writeRunPostmortem (board.ts) hardcodes validation_issue_count:0 and omits total_issue_count; writeZeroRoundRunPostmortem (index.ts) derives validation_issue_count correctly and includes total_issue_count but omits review_issue_count. The same governed artifact has two divergent PM-003 shapes depending on exit path. COD-009 compliance is only partial — the block was added but the canonical-shape requirement is unmet.
improvements_applied: Self-check issues now preserve their original ValidationIssue severity through board execution and result synthesis instead of being flattened to major or error.
Board run-postmortem artifacts now include the PM-003 issue-count block, bringing zero-round and normal/blocked board closeout onto the same question_outputs shape.
Learning-engine output and component-repair-engine compliance/fix-item arrays are now schema-validated at the boundary before they can update governed artifacts.
Result synthesis no longer upgrades warning-level self-check issues into error severity, so red_flags reflect real error-severity findings only.
The redundant cycle-postmortem existence check and the unused validator mdLower variable were removed.
